places.name: Vito & Nick's
places.address: 8433 S Pulaski Rd, Chicago, IL, 60652, US
places.blurb: Pizza enthusiasts flock to this revered stalwart on the far South Side for some of the finest cracker-like, thin-crust pies in the city. The crispy dough is topped with a plethora of ingredients, ranging from shrimp and anchovies to sliced beef and sausage. It’s best paired with a cold pint of Old Style. Bring some bills as the restaurant is cash-only.

places.name: Phil’s Pizza
places.address: 1102 W 35th St, Chicago, IL, 60609, US
places.blurb: Rarely is tavern-style pizza made with the care and attention found at Phil’s, located just steps from Sox Park in Bridgeport. For fans of sausage- or Italian beef-topped pizzas, Phil’s is unmatched. The pizzas keep well, too — don’t worry about reheating them at home after a long drive. This spot is cash-only.

places.name: Williams Inn Pizza & Sports Bar
places.address: 2210 S Michigan Ave, Chicago, IL, 60616, US
places.blurb: A longtime South Side staple, Williams Inn Pizza & Sports Bar left Englewood after a fire and relocated to the South Loop in 2021. Owner Lulu Williams developed the dough and secret sauce recipe in the late ’60s, building a loyal following that continues to this day. While thin crust is available, most people order the deep dish. The sausage version delivers classic Chicago flavor with a thick, buttery crust baked crisp — not doughy — and a carefully balanced ratio of sauce, cheese, and toppings. It’s rich without tipping into overly heavy, the kind of deep dish you’ll think about long after the last bite. Williams Inn is primarily first-come, first-served, though reservations can be arranged by phone. Online orders are accepted.

places.name: Novel Pizza Cafe
places.address: 1759 W 19th St, Chicago, IL, 60608, US
places.blurb: Novel Pizza Cafe, nestled into a residential corner near Harrison Park in Pilsen, is a nostalgic spot featuring vintage sports memorabilia and a handful of tables. This is a tribute to Filipino and Mexican immigrants. The signature tavern-style pizza is topped with longganisa and giardiniera, providing a sweet and spicy kick. Look out for rotating specials. There’s also Chicago pan pizza. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Bonci
places.address: 161 N Sangamon St, Chicago, IL, 60607, US
places.blurb: Pizza craftsman Gabriele Bonci introduced his world-famous restaurant to the U.S. in 2017 with a West Loop location. The Roman-style pizza al taglio — baked in large rectangular trays and sold by weight — has a focaccia-like crust and ever-changing topping combinations that include cured meats, cheeses, and other seasonal ingredients. Online ordering is available. There are also locations in Lakeview and Lincoln Park.

places.name: Pizz’Amici
places.address: 1215 W Grand Ave, Chicago, IL, 60642, US
places.blurb: Pizz’amici opened at the end of 2024, but this West Town spot has already cemented itself as one of the city’s best pizzerias. The super-thin pies were originally showcased to the masses at Bridgeport’s Pizza Fried Chicken Ice Cream before heading to the suburbs at Westmont’s Kim’s Uncle Pizza. Cecily and Billy Federighi eventually left the operation to open their own restaurant, Pizz’amici. This is one of the toughest reservations in town, but worth it. Carryout ordering is available.

places.name: Zarella Pizzeria & Taverna
places.address: 531 N Wells St, Chicago, IL, 60654, US
places.blurb: Expectations were high when one of the city’s most prominent restaurant groups entered the pizza game, but Boka delivered with Zarella. Led by chefs Chris Pandel and Lee Wolen, the River North restaurant offers a warm, inviting setting for enjoying both tavern- and “artisan-style” pies in creative varieties like spicy vodka, spinach and artichoke, and carbonara. Secure a seat in the subterranean speakeasy lounge for a more intimate experience. Place orders online for takeout.

places.name: Milly’s Pizza In The Pan
places.address: 925 North Ashland Avenue, Chicago, Illinois 60622, United States
places.blurb: In 2020, Robert Maleski plunged into pizza making and launched a virtual restaurant before eventually moving into a permanent home in Uptown in early 2022, and later relocating to West Town in 2025. His take on pan pizza is inspired by Chicago legend Burt Katz (Burt’s Place) and features a ring of caramelized mozzarella around the dough. The crunchy crust holds lots of cheese and tomato sauce; it’s adorned with topping combinations like Que Suerte (pineapple, cherry tomatoes, jalapeño, brown sugar bacon) and Craigslist.org (peppadew peppers, sausage, mushrooms, red onions, tomatoes, spinach, ricotta). All pies are also finished with dollops of fresh mozzarella. Orders can be placed online. A second location is open in suburban Berwyn.

places.name: Piece Brewery & Pizzeria
places.address: 1927 West North Avenue, IL, 60622
places.blurb: The place is usually packed, and there’s often a wait, but it’s easy to understand why people love the pies at Piece. The New Haven-style thin-crust pizzas can be customized with red or white sauce and topped with an enormous list of eclectic ingredients, including mashed potatoes, artichoke hearts, broccoli, baby-neck clams, and meatballs. Also check out the special pizzas featuring Doug Sohn’s (Hot Doug’s) Atomic Sausage and Honey Butter’s fried chicken. Place orders online for carryout.

places.name: Sharpies
places.address: 1721 N Elston Ave, Chicago, IL, 60642, US
places.blurb: Sharpies is a Detroit-style pizzeria offering some of the best pies in Chicago — squares so good that they could give some of the Motor City’s best competition. The restaurant, from the owners of Ada Street, emphasizes technique. The cosmic pizzas are as eclectic as their names. From the Drooling Mushroom Eclipse(bella mushroom, garlic, parsley, a lemon compound butter, red chile flake, and Parmesan) to the Sausage Singularity (fennel sausage, Calabrian chiles, and ricotta). Place online orders for pickup.

places.name: Bungalow by Middle Brow
places.address: 2840 West Armitage Avenue, IL, 60647
places.blurb: Pizza and beer are an immaculate pairing and also the main attractions at this Logan Square brewpub. Focusing on fermentation, Bungalow by Middle Brow makes exceptional breads along with wild and experimental brews. The thin, chewy crust is activated with a sourdough starter, which gives the dough a pleasant tanginess. Toppings are sourced from local farms, and the lineup includes options like margherita, mushroom, and the weekly specials. On Tuesdays, Middle Brow offers tavern-style pizza that’s worth a trip. Place online orders with Toast.

places.name: Dimmi Dimmi Corner Italian
places.address: 1112 W Armitage Ave, Chicago, IL, 60614, US
places.blurb: Lincoln Park welcomed a new red-sauce Italian restaurant in 2025, taking over the former home of neighborhood favorite Tarantino’s. Since opening, it’s been packed nightly — due in large part to chef Matt Eckfeld’s tavern-style pizzas. The cracker-thin crust holds up a sweet tomato sauce and classic toppings like sausage and pepperoni, while the vodka-sauce pie stands out with sweet onions, ’nduja, and Calabrian chile. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Paulie Gee's
places.address: 2451 N Milwaukee Ave, Chicago, IL, 60647, US
places.blurb: The Brooklyn-based pizzeria arrived in Logan Square in 2016 with lots of hype surrounding its Neapolitan-influenced offerings, and managed to live up to those lofty expectations. The wood-fired creations include Greenpointer (mozzarella, baby arugula, olive oil, lemon juice, and shaved Parmigiano reggiano) and Hellboy (mozzarella, Italian tomatoes, Berkshire soppressata picante, Parmigiano reggiano, and Mike’s Hot Honey). There’s a vegan menu that uses dairy-free cheese, plus Detroit-style squares with cup and char pepperoni. Paulie Gee’s is first-come, first-served, and they don’t accept phone calls, so place orders for pickup with Toast. P.S. They have a Wicker Park location that focuses on New York-style slices. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Pat's Pizza
places.address: 2679 N Lincoln Ave, Chicago, IL, 60614, US
places.blurb: Pat’s thin and crispy crust is so good that it would make a South Sider jealous. This might be the best tavern pie in town. The sausage is made in-house and always has a satisfying bite. The crusts are impossibly thin, and the family-owned restaurant has become an expert at the art of consistency. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Zazas Pizzeria
places.address: 3037 N Clark St, Chicago, IL, 60657, US
places.blurb: Zazas has built a following with New York-style pizza that falls into its own category. The crust is thin, chewy, and foldable — topped with ingredients like truffled mushrooms, Calabrian chile, and spicy Caruso giardiniera. The menu includes a bacon jam pie (red onions, Calabrian chile, cotija cheese, and scallions), a white pie (garlic oil, whipped ricotta, mozzarella, basil, and sesame seed crust), and a truffle mushroom pie (cremini mushrooms, rosemary, garlic oil, truffle butter, and goat cheese). Grandma-style pizzas are also available, and the restaurant is BYOB. Place orders online.

places.name: The Beer Temple
places.address: 3173 North Elston Avenue, IL, 60618
places.blurb: Avondale bottle shop and taproom the Beer Temple teamed up with Middle Brow to create a Detroit-style pizza for its menu. The crust, made from Middle Brow’s sourdough starter, sports crisp, caramelized edges. Tangy red sauce and Wisconsin brick cheese form the base, finished with a sprinkle of grated Parmesan. Toppings range from classics like sausage, pepperoni, and mushrooms to specials such as paprika chicken and Brussels sprouts. Slices are also available. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Forno Rosso
places.address: 3719 N Harlem Ave, Chicago, IL, 60634, US
places.blurb: Forno Rosso follows the strict guidelines set in Naples for Neapolitan pizza, ensuring consistently excellent pies made with ingredients imported directly from Italy. The Datterino, winner of the Caputo Cup — an international pizza-making competition — features creamy mozzarella di bufala, sweet red piennolo tomatoes, and basil, finished with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil. Reservations are accepted — just keep in mind that each table is limited to 90 minutes. A second Forno Rosso is located in the West Loop and is pickup only.

places.name: Crust Fund Pizza
places.address: Chicago, IL
places.blurb: Crust Fund Pizza is the little pop-up that could. Founded by home chef John Carruthers, it’s his way of sharing his love for pizza while raising funds for charitable causes. He takes orders via Instagram (drops happen monthly). Customers don’t pay Carruthers, though; they donate money to a charity of his choice in exchange for a pie picked up at a secret back-alley location. The pizza is mostly Chicago thin crust, but look for a variety of specials. And be quick to lock in an order as drops sell out instantly. Sign up for the mailing list to get the pizza drops days before everyone else.

places.name: Spacca Napoli
places.address: 1769 W Sunnyside Ave, Chicago, IL, 60640, US
places.blurb: This Ravenswood destination is always busy at night with a dining room full of hungry customers waiting for an experience similar to what one would expect in Italy. The Neapolitan pies are fired quickly and served hot. There’s plenty to try, from the Conciato with smoked mozzarella and squash cream to a pie topped with pistachio cream, basil, and sausage. Reservations can be made by giving the restaurant a call, and walk-ins are always welcome.

places.name: Jimmy’s Pizza Cafe
places.address: 2434 W Montrose Ave, Chicago, IL, 60618, US
places.blurb: Chicagoans can appreciate good pizza, even if it’s New York-style. Jimmy’s features crispy, chewy slices that beg to be folded and scarfed down. Call it the Great Unifier. Jimmy’s moved to a larger location in early 2022 and expanded its dine-in seating capacity. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Barnaby's
places.address: 960 Skokie Blvd, Northbrook, IL 60062, USA
places.blurb: What was once a prominent local chain has been whittled down to a just a few locations in the suburbs. But that hasn’t stopped this Northbrook outpost from cranking out superb tavern-style pizzas. The crisp, cornmeal-dusted crust provides the foundation for a right amount of cheese, tangy sauce, and toppings like house-made sausage.

places.name: Candlelite
places.address: 7452 N Western Ave, Chicago, IL 60645, USA
places.blurb: It’s hard to miss the gigantic neon arrow on Western Avenue pointing folks in the direction of Candlelite. West Rogers Park is home to great cracker-thin-crust pizzas featuring ingredients such as Italian beef, shrimp, and barbecue chicken. On Tuesdays, those who dine in can get two pies for the price of one. A second location at 1501 N. Kingsbury Street in Lincoln Park has opened inside the iO Theater and offers the same menu, including the skillet ice cream cookie. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Michael's Original Pizzeria & Tavern
places.address: 4091 N Broadway St, Chicago, IL 60613, USA
places.blurb: Head to Uptown for cold beer and flaky and buttery crusts topped with a thick layer of ingredients. Michael’s is a sports bar that does Chicago-style pizza the way it was meant to be done. If guests prefer to hold the tomato sauce, an extra thin white pie finished with garlic, olive oil, mozzarella, provolone, and Parmesan is the ideal alternative. Online ordering is available.

places.name: La Villa
places.address: 3632 N Pulaski Rd, Chicago, IL 60641, USA
places.venue_title: La Villa Restaurant Lounge and Banquets
places.blurb: Exuding Old World charm, this family-run establishment has been a steady presence on the Northwest Side for decades. The kitchen makes everything from scratch, and the top-notch thin-crusts feature a second layer of cheese over the toppings. Selections include the La Villa Special (sausage, pepperoni, green pepper, mushroom, green olives, onion), Veggie Alfredo, and Southwestern (barbecue sauce, chicken, bacon). Online ordering is available.

places.name: WG Pizzas
places.address: 2819 North Southport Avenue, Chicago, Illinois 60657, United States
places.blurb: This Lakeview restaurant is a spin-off of Alex’s Washington Gardens in suburban Highwood, which might be the oldest tavern-style pizzeria in Lake County. The crust isn’t razor thin, but proudly retains a few bubbles on the ends. So while it’s crispy, it’s a little chewier and reminds you of many South Side spots (without the signature sweeter sauce). Online ordering is available.

places.name: Pat's Pizzeria & Ristorante
places.address: 2679 N Lincoln Ave, Chicago, IL 60614, USA
places.blurb: The self-proclaimed “No. 1 thin-crust pizza” in Chicago has legions of fans who agree. The cracker-like crust is impossibly thin and a perfect base for hunks of house-made sausage. Pat’s is family-owned and very consistent. It may not have frosted glasses or look like a revered institution, but the friendly dining room serves some of the best pies in Chicago. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Bungalow by Middle Brow
places.address: 2840 W Armitage Ave, Chicago, IL 60647, USA
places.blurb: Middlebrow in Logan Square is a brewery and winery that focuses on beers produced with wild yeast and wines made with minimum intervention. It’s a far cry from an Old Style and a square, but this more modern pairing works with all of its pies. Tuesdays are when tavern pizzas are available. They’re remarkably thin and take a more gourmet approach. You can get sausage and pepperoni anywhere, but pizzas topped with vegetables from the region’s top farms, unconventional sauces, and collaborations with restaurants are more interesting. Regardless, fans of thin-crust should make it a point to try Middlebrow because it might just become their new favorite. Book a table on Resy.

places.name: Pizza Matta
places.address: 3211 West Armitage Avenue, Chicago, Illinois 60647, United States
places.blurb: The team behind Giant and Chef’s Special run this small Logan Square pizzeria that specializes in New York and tavern-style pies. Though there are a few pre-configured topping combinations, diners are encouraged to build their own 16-inch pizzas with a host of options. There are red, white, and green sauces for the base plus ingredients like Slagel Farm Italian sausage, mortadella, Italian beef, eggplant, spicy banana peppers, clams, and much more. Don’t skip the salads, either. Seating is limited so plan ahead. Pizza Matta has also collaborated with local pizza maker Billy Zureikat in the past to offer Chicago Squares inspired by Bears’ opponents throughout the season. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Professor Pizza
places.address: 3220 West Grand Avenue, IL 60651
places.blurb: Professor Pizza has found a permanent home next to Second City. Anthony Scardino, known as “Professor Pizza,” is a competitive pizza maker who mashes up styles and creates a great variety of different pizzas. The tavern-style keeps up a high level of quality. Listed as “cracker-thin,” the deluxe pepperoni is dotted with hot honey and whipped ricotta. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Zarella Pizzeria & Taverna
places.address: 531 N Wells St, Chicago, IL, 60654, US
places.blurb: Boka Restaurant Group transformed the former GT Fish & Oyster into a sophisticated bar and restaurant that happens to serve “artisan” and tavern-style pizzas. The company brought in their big gun chefs to R&D the crusts — they’re dusted with cornmeal and resemble the pies slung at Crust Fund Pizza. This is River North, so you’ll pay River North prices (pepperoni is listed as a “fancy” ingredient), but Boka goes above and beyond, and you can pay using the QR codes on the table. It’s a fun reimagination of the classic pizzeria. Reservations are available on OpenTable.

places.name: Pizz’Amici
places.address: 1215 W Grand Ave, Chicago, IL, 60642, US
places.blurb: Pizz’amici was one of the biggest openings of 2024, as two of the former partners in Pizza Fried Chicken Ice Cream and Kim’s Uncle Pizza launched their own restaurant in West Town. Fans of those previous establishments know what to expect: A crispy and thin crust that’s a good representation of the new wave of tavern-style pizza sweeping through town. Reservations need to be booked well in advance, but know that carryout is easy if you think ahead and don’t need a pizza in the next hour or so.

places.name: Crust Fund Pizza
places.address: Chicago, IL, USA
places.blurb: Crust Fund Pizza isn’t just about cool logos that may have been inspired by Cuphead, the impossibly challenging video game. It’s not even a restaurant. It’s a force for good from John Carruthers, an obsessive home cook with connections to the restaurant scene. Carruthers has worked hard to perfect his thin-crust pies, which are offered in very limited availability via Instagram on a regular basis. Carruthers asks customers to donate money to a not-for-profit group (the selection changes). It’s a clever way to raise funds while celebrating some of the best pizza in town. There are frequent collaborations with characters like food writer and pizza chef Dennis Lee, and pies are picked up in a back alley on the North Side. This is a mighty pop-up that embraces what it means to be a Chicagoan.

places.name: Novel Pizza Cafe
places.address: 1759 W 19th St, Chicago, IL, 60608, US
places.blurb: Drawing inspiration from the Filipino and Mexican heritages of its owners, Novel Pizza Cafe was an immediate hit when it opened in 2024. The Pilsen pizzeria offers tavern-style and pan pies, with the highlight being the longganisa (Filipino sausage) and giardiniera combo — a harmonious balance of savoriness, spiciness, and sweetness. There’s also a selection of coffee beverages, pastries from Fat Peach on weekends, and pizzas by the slice. Novel tends to sell out due to its popularity so ordering early is recommended.

places.name: Villa Nova
places.address: 6821 Pershing Rd, Stickney, IL 60402, USA
places.blurb: Another contender for the thin-crust crown, Villa Nova is a no-frills spot that deserves to be mentioned among the South Side’s greats. It’s worth a trip out to suburban Stickney to enjoy the square-cut pizzas topped with juicy house sausage — left in chunks that resemble meatballs — gooey mozzarella, and other add-ons like Italian beef and chicken. There’s also a location across the border in New Buffalo, Michigan. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Kim’s Uncle Pizza
places.address: 207 North Cass Avenue, Westmont, Illinois 60559
places.blurb: Kim’s Uncle Pizza serves the same pizza that Pizza Fried Chicken Ice Cream, which was attached to Maria’s Community Bar in Bridgeport, did. Located in Westmont, it’s one of the best restaurants in the suburbs — a tiny spot that specializes in carryout. This pizza has received national write-ups.

places.name: Obbie's Pizza
places.address: 6654 W Archer Ave, Chicago, IL 60638, USA
places.blurb: Garfield Ridge residents know and love this quaint carryout joint. The neighborhood favorite has received accolades (including an appearance on The Good Wife) for one of the best thin-crust pizzas the South Side has to offer. They’re sauced all the way to the edges, giving each bite a nice balance, and cooked in a 1947 Middleby Marshall oven. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Palermo's of 63rd
places.address: 3751 W 63rd St, Chicago, IL 60629, USA
places.blurb: This treasured Italian restaurant (unaffiliated with the one in Oak Lawn) has been satisfying customers for over six decades. The quality thin-crust pies, served in an old-school dining room, feature a distinctive sweet tomato sauce that sets it apart from the competition.

places.name: Italian Fiesta
places.address: 1919 E 71st St, Chicago, IL 60649, USA
places.blurb: Opened in the late ‘40s, Italian Fiesta is a popular South Side staple with five locations. The pizza is also a favorite of President Obama and was served at his 2009 inauguration. It’s carryout-only and customers should expect to wait at least 45 minutes, but the reward is a solid thin-crust pie with a buttery crust, lots of topping choices, and a hefty amount of oregano.

places.name: Vito & Nick's
places.address: 8433 S Pulaski Rd, Chicago, IL 60652, USA
places.blurb: The institution has long been praised as the king of tavern-style pizza. In a town where deep dish gets all the buzz, Vito & Nick’s still manages to garner national recognition for its thin, crunchy crusts, topped with everything from fennel sausage and ground beef to shrimp and anchovies. It’s a must-visit not just for pizza lovers, but every Chicagoan. Make sure to bring cash and order a pint of Old Style.

places.name: Uncle Jerry's Pizza Company
places.address: 133 W Main St, Cary, IL 60013, USA
places.blurb: Cary is a town about an hour’s drive northwest of Chicago and Uncle Jerry’s provides city-dwellers with a compelling reason to visit. The pizzeria stands inside a butcher’s shop; Orchard Prime Meats will cut steaks to order. It also provides Uncle Jerry’s with fresh sausage made on-premises. The pizza owes much to Lou Malnati’s — not too thick, not too thin. The sauce is slightly sweet without as much pulp. The team uses cupped pepperoni, a rarity in the deep-dish game, and the Bee Sting is glazed with habanero honey. The overall ambiance and quality of the pies make the trip worth it for Chicagoans who love deep dish. There’s also a good beer selection.

places.name: Burt's Place
places.address: 8541 Ferris Ave, Morton Grove, IL 60053, USA
places.blurb: Before Burt Katz died in 2016, the eccentric pizza maker impressed Anthony Bourdain in a 2009 No Reservations segment. Bourdain claimed this was the deep dish that legitimized the form. Katz’s failing health forced a closure. Reopened in 2017 under new ownership, Burt’s Place continues to turn out Katz’s renowned pan pie with caramelized crust, a recipe he also introduced at Pequod’s. Katz was known for his commitment to quality ingredients and a focus on small details. This new era of Burt’s continues the legacy. Order ahead here.

places.name: George's Deep Dish
places.address: 6221 N Clark St, Chicago, IL 60660, USA
places.blurb: George’s Deep Dish in Edgewater puts a new spin on pizza by taking inspiration from Greek pastry. The pies feature premium ingredients and whimsical combos. The edge of the crust is caramelized, and will likely spoil other deep dishes for diners. Once you try George’s is hard to go back to the status quo. The Halas’ Classic, named after the Chicago Bears’ legendary coach and owner, is a fan favorite. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Bartoli's
places.address: 1955 W Addison St, Chicago, IL 60613, USA
places.blurb: Bartoli’s owner Brian Tondryk comes from an esteemed family of pizza experts. His grandfather was a founder of Gino’s East, so it’s no surprise that Tondryk’s pies are similarly remarkable, boasting a light, flaky crust and layers of mild mozzarella and sweet red sauce. There are several versions to choose from, but Nonna Bartoli’s special spinach and cheese pizza is a standout. There’s a second location in West Town. Pizzas are also available by the slice, which makes for a great and quick lunch. Place an online order here.

places.name: The Art of Pizza
places.address: 3033 N Ashland Ave, Chicago, IL 60657, USA
places.blurb: This Lakeview classic on Ashland Avenue is still chugging along and one of the few places that sells deep-dish slices. Art of Pizza continues to hold onto a strong fanbase, and its deep dish features a flaky and buttery crust. Order online here.

places.name: Kitchen 17
places.address: 2554 W Diversey Ave, Chicago, IL 60647, USA
places.blurb: For vegans, Kitchen 17 is life as it’s one of the few to offer deep-dish vegan pizza. This is actually the third iteration of the restaurant, which moved from Lakeview to Avondale at the end of 2021. The vegan cheese is made on-premises with soy. Pro tip: Get an after-dinner beer at Diversey House across the street or Maplewood Brewery. Place a delivery or takeout order here.

places.name: Pequod's
places.address: 2207 N Clybourn Ave, Chicago, IL 60614, USA
places.blurb: Pequod’s is best known for its caramelized crust. The airy dough is baked with a thin layer of cheese, producing crispy edges and a bit of chewiness. It acts as the perfect base for toppings like sausage, Italian beef, meatballs, and giardiniera. Individual-sized pan pizzas are available for those dining solo. Click here to place an order for carryout or delivery. There’s another location in suburban Morton Grove.

places.name: Milly's Pizza In The Pan
places.address: 925 N Ashland Ave, Chicago, IL 60622
places.blurb: Robert Maleski began making and selling pizza as a pandemic project out of a Logan Square ghost kitchen. His deep-dish pies with a ring of caramelized cheese baked into the crust were quickly recognized as some of the city’s best so in 2022, Maleski eventually moved into a permanent space with seating — first in Uptown before recently relocating to Noble Square. Milly’s makes a limited number of pies each day. The pizza is similar to Pequod’s, but more consistent because Maleski doesn’t deal with such a high volume. A second location is open in suburban Berwyn. Order ahead because it tends to sell out.

places.name: Pizano's Pizza & Pasta
places.address: 864 N State St, Chicago, IL 60610, USA
places.blurb: The history of deep-dish pizza is intrinsically linked to one Chicago family — the Malnatis. Rudy Malnati Sr., a longtime employee at Pizzeria Uno, is credited with helping to invent the style. His sons would go on to start a pizza empire by opening Lou Malnati’s and this smaller mini chain, Pizano’s. What sets the pies here apart from the competition is the dough, which was developed by owner Rudy Malnati Jr.’s late mother, Donna Marie Malnati. The namesake “Rudy’s Special” is a harmonious marriage of that golden crust with sausage, mushrooms, onions, and green peppers. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Gino's East
places.address: 162 E Superior St, Chicago, IL 60611, USA
places.blurb: Dating all the way back to 1966, Gino’s East is one of the city’s most celebrated deep-dish specialists. Expect a heavy helping of mozzarella and tangy tomato sauce atop cornmeal crust made from a secret recipe. Menu highlights include the Diavola (spicy pepperoni, basil, white truffle oil) and the Chicago Fire (spicy sausage, roasted red peppers, red onions). To order delivery or pickup, click here.

places.name: Giordano’s
places.address: 730 N Rush St, Chicago, IL, 60611, US
places.blurb: One of Chicago’s most well-known pizza chains, Giordano’s is for those who can’t get enough cheese. The signature stuffed pizza puts a twist on classic deep dish, stacking mozzarella and toppings beneath a second layer of crust, then smothering it all in tomato sauce to create massive, towering slices. The menu features playful topping combinations inspired by dishes like the gyro and the Juicy Lucy burger. The hearty pizzas aren’t for everyone, but diehards swear by Giordano’s. Place an online order for pickup or delivery.

places.name: Labriola
places.address: 535 North Michigan Avenue, Chicago, Illinois 60611
places.blurb: The Magnificent Mile caters to out-of-towners with chains, so it’s refreshing to find a place for deep dish that’s not a tourist trap. Labriola’s focaccia-style dough is layered with mozzarella before baking, creating a crunchy, caramelized crust reminiscent of favorites like Pequod’s and Milly’s. Guests can create their own pies but our go-to is Danny’s Special — Italian sausage with green peppers, onions, and mushrooms. Pair it with a spritz on the umbrella-shaded patio and might just forget you’re steps away from the bustle of Michigan Avenue. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Lou Malnati's
places.address: 1120 N State St, Chicago, IL 60610, USA
places.blurb: Chicago’s most ubiquitous pizza chain is also one of its finest. Opened in 1971, Lou Malnati’s has helped spread the popularity of deep dish across the city and country. The Malnati clan claims to have had a hand in the dish’s creation and its signature “Malnati Chicago Classic” — sausage, cheese, and tomato sauce on a special butter crust — still sets the standard for an excellent pie. Lou’s also has some newish menu items like a lower-carb sausage crust. Order online here.

places.name: Nancy's Pizzeria
places.address: 1000 W Washington Blvd, Chicago, IL 60607, USA
places.blurb: Rocco Palese invented stuffed pizza at Guy’s Pizza in Hermosa, but when he opened his own place, which eventually grew into a chain, he named it after his wife Nancy. Each pie is two-and-a-half inches tall and packed with tons of cheese, sauce, toppings, and two layers of dough. Folks can customize their own ingredients or opt for one of the greatest hits, such as the Spicy Pepino (pepperoni, giardiniera, banana peppers). Place online orders here.

places.name: Beatrix Market
places.address: 23 E Jackson Blvd, Chicago, IL 60604, USA
places.blurb: Beatrix Market is a clutch spot for quick grab-and-go drinks and sandwiches near DePaul University’s Loop campus. But what if we told you that this location, part of the Lettuce Entertain You Enterprises’ empire, also serves personal-sized deep-dish pizzas in cheese, pepperoni, sausage, or veggie? They cost less than $11 and are impressive. Eighteen-inch pies are also available.

places.name: Williams Inn Pizza & Sports Bar
places.address: 2210 S Michigan Ave, Chicago, IL 60616, USA
places.blurb: Williams Inn is a sports bar that opened in 2021 in the South Loop with a historic name. The owners’ stuffed pizza was first served at a restaurant that burned down in 2010. But they’ve rebuilt and the pizza has returned. And it’s delicious. The establishment dates back to 1969 and now has a bar so it’s a popular spot to watch the big game. Order online here.

places.name: Angelo's Stuffed Pizza
places.address: 4850 S Pulaski Rd, Chicago, IL 60632, USA
places.blurb: Angelo’s got a bit of attention in 2021 when it collaborated with its Archer Heights neighbor Birrieria Zaragoza on a goat birria-stuffed pizza. The pizza itself drew mixed reactions and is no longer available, but Angelo’s still allows customers to experiment with toppings like frijoles and nopales. And, like all great Chicago pizzerias, it offers a “well done” option for customers who like their crust extra crispy. Place an order online.

places.name: Pizza Capri
places.address: 1501 E 53rd St, Chicago, IL, 60615, US
places.blurb: Pizza Capri, with locations in Hyde Park and Avondale, continues to be one of the best spots for stuffed pizza. Expect a cheesy and saucy slab with buttery crust and an array of toppings not always found at pizzerias like artichoke hearts, gorgonzola, and even shrimp. It’s been serving Chicago for more than two decades. Gluten-free pizzas are also available. Order online here.

places.name: Nino’s Pizza
places.address: 4835 West 111th Street, Alsip, Illinois 60803, United States
places.blurb: Alsip locals know to head to Nino’s for the area’s most impressive deep dish. The pie’s tall, flaky crust forms a thick wall around a gooey mound of cheese and acidic tomato sauce. Getting it with sausage is highly recommended.

places.name: Louisa's Pizza & Pasta
places.address: 14025 S Cicero Ave, Crestwood, IL 60445, USA
places.blurb: Pizzaiola Louisa DeGenero honed her skills at Pizzeria Due before relocating to the South Suburbs in 1981. Today, her family carries on her legacy with pies that are a paragon of the form. The crisp and buttery crust is complemented by hunks of Anichini sausage, part-skim mozzarella, and bright and chunky tomato sauce seasoned with homegrown herbs. Online ordering is available.

places.name: Pizza Pushers
places.address: 644 South Main Street, Algonquin, Illinois 60102, United States
places.blurb: Three restaurant vets partnered to open this pizzeria in the Northwest Suburbs. Pizza Pushers uses a focaccia base for its dough, resulting in the light and airy crust that Detroit style is known for. In addition to traditional toppings like pepperoni and sausage, the restaurant also offers pies with vodka cream sauce and pickled jalapeños. Even for city dwellers, it’s worth a commute.

places.name: Union Squared
places.address: 1307 Chicago Avenue, IL 60201
places.blurb: This Evanston pizzeria was among the first in the Chicago metro area to specialize in Motor City pies. Inspired by the style’s birthplace, Buddy’s Pizza in Detroit, Union co-founder Vince DiBattista sought to introduce it to a new audience. The hallmarks are all evident on Union’s offerings: crisp edges, brick cheese, and tomato sauce ladled on top. Topping combinations include broccoli and potato, burrata margherita, and buffalo chicken and blue cheese, and gluten-free and vegan cheese are available upon request. The restaurant also has a lovely back patio.

places.name: Fat Chris’s Pizza and Such
places.address: 1706 W Foster Ave, Chicago, IL, 60640, US
places.blurb: For those who really want to have it their way, this Andersonville restaurant obliges. Customize your Detroit-style pizzas with a host of toppings like pepperoni, Canadian bacon, roast beef, pineapple, spinach, and more, along with the option of gluten-free crust. Can’t decide? There’s no going wrong with the specialty pies — the Big Tex features barbecue sauce, brisket, onions, green peppers, and perfectly caramelized edges. The pepperoni rolls are also a favorite.

places.name: The Beer Temple
places.address: 3173 North Elston Avenue, IL, 60618
places.blurb: The Beer Temple is a craft beer bar in Avondale that searched for the ideal partner to provide food service. They teamed with Middle Brow, the beer maker known for its artisanal pizza, and came up with a Detroit-style pizza with a remarkable chassis that has a little bit more funk and sourdough taste (not overpowering) than traditional pies. The dough and ingredients are top notch, and the toppings are traditional with pepperoni cups, sausage, and giardiniera. Tip: There’s an awkward and tiny parking lot behind the bar. Make use of it.

places.name: Paulie Gee’s Logan Square
places.address: 2451 N Milwaukee Ave, Chicago, IL, 60647, US
places.blurb: When Paulie Gee’s made its way to Chicago in 2016, it didn’t just bring the Neapolitan-style pies the Brooklyn pizzeria is known for. Owner Derrick Tung also introduced Detroit-style “Logan Squares” to the menu, and what a good decision that turned out to be. The best-selling U.S. Pizza Cup Winner is a sweet-salty-crunchy medley of charred pepperoni cups, bacon jam, ricotta, and hot honey. As the name implies, it took home the top prize at the 2018 U.S. Pizza Cup (we won’t disagree with that decision). A gluten-free version is so ridiculous that many won’t be able to tell the difference and surpasses gluten-free options in the Motor City, especially the version from that popular chain.

places.name: Sharpies
places.address: 1721 N Elston Ave, Chicago, IL, 60642, US
places.blurb: Sharpies’s opening was a big deal in Chicago as it showed locals were familiar enough with Detroit squares to try toppings beyond pepperoni and sausage. The crust at Sharpies doesn’t skimp on butter, so you get a delightful crunch that’s not just reserved for corner pieces. The bacon, pineapple, goat cheese, and sambal (Sweet & Salty Spicy Candy) and arugula, balsamic glaze (Sharpies Beef) star. There are also great cocktails and stellar wings. It’s also worth investing an interest in the mascot’s crazy backstory while bringing some quarters for a few rounds of NBA Jam.

places.name: Professor Pizza
places.address: 1610 N. Wells Street, Chicago, IL
places.blurb: Tony Scardino, a competitive pizza maker affectionately known as “Professor Pizza,” started slinging pizzas to the public out of a Wrigleyville pop-up during the pandemic, eventually moving into a ghost kitchen. In 2024, he found a home in Old Town, replacing Roots Handmade Pizza. At Professor Pizza, folks can try Motor City pies along with New York, tavern, Sicilian, and Grandma-style. The deluxe pepperoni is the move here: crispy, charred pepperoni cups with dollops of whipped ricotta and hot honey.

places.name: Five Squared Pizza
places.address: 1332 North Halsted Street, Chicago, Illinois 60642
places.blurb: Jenn and Steve Kaplan quit their day jobs and started this pizzeria in 2019. Operating out of a ghost kitchen near Goose Island, the couple offer Detroit squares in rotating flavors like spinach artichoke, burrata caprese, and smash burger. Each mix-and-match box comes with four slices to be reheated at home. Five Squared is takeout- and delivery-only, but it also serves hot pies at Off Color Brewing and Midwest Coast Brewing Thursdays through Saturdays.

places.name: Five Star Bar
places.address: 1424 W Chicago Ave, Chicago, IL, 60642, US
places.blurb: West Town’s Five Star Bar returned from its pandemic closure in 2021 with new management and a revamped menu. Owner Daniel Macielak brought over the recipes from his Michigan pizzeria, so patrons can enjoy some squares with their beers and cocktails. There’s the usual selection of pepperoni, sausage, and veggie options, but for something a bit different, try the pie featuring tikka masala, tandoori chicken, sweet peppers, and mango sweet chili.

places.name: Red's the People's Pie at Rock Island Public House
places.address: 13328 Olde Western Ave, Blue Island, IL, 60406, US
places.blurb: Head to Rock Island Public House in Blue Island on Sundays and Tuesdays to catch Red’s the People’s Pie, a tavern- and Detroit-style pizza pop-up. Pabst Blue Ribbon is used in the dough and the ever-changing menu has offered pies topped with Italian beef, meatballs, sriracha, and more.

places.name: Cloverleaf Bar & Restaurant
places.address: 24443 Gratiot Ave, Eastpointe, MI 48021, USA
places.blurb: This Eastpointe pizzeria was established by Buddy’s founder Gus Guerra and thus also claims to serve a version of the original Detroit-style pizza recipe. Per tradition, the pizzas at Cloverleaf are baked in a high-walled, rectangular pan in order to amplify the crunchy and cheesy corners.

places.name: Belle Isle Pizza
places.address: 7869 E Jefferson Ave, Detroit, MI, 48214, US
places.blurb: West Village’s carryout shop Belle Isle Pizza serves hand-tossed, thin-crust, and Detroit-style pizza. The menu features a variety of Detroit-themed signature pies, including the 7 Villages, made with a fresh-herb ricotta sauce, mozzarella, fontina, and parmesan cheeses, gourmet mushrooms medley, arugula, and olive oil.

places.name: Village Pizza
places.address: 7701 Harper Avenue, Detroit, Michigan 48213
places.blurb: Village Pizza is a plant-based option for Detroit-style pizza. The brainchild of Brittany March, the pizzas are prepared by local youth at the Alkebu-lan Village, a nonprofit organization on the city’s east side, where it’s available for pickup every Sunday. Customers can place orders online beginning on Wednesday of each week and select a time slot to grab it. Choose between a six-slice small or a 10-slice large and pick one of the specialties, like the Gratiot Pesto, prepared with freshly made pesto, artichoke, red onion, peppers, roasted mushrooms, and tomatoes. Craving that cheese pull? The vegan cheese bread comes highly recommended. For those in your party who just can’t go without meat, the pizzeria also has a brief carnivore menu, with combinations that feature turkey-based pepperoni, chicken, and dairy cheese.

places.name: Buddy’s Pizza
places.address: 17125 Conant St, Detroit, MI, 48212, US
places.blurb: When people say Detroit-style square pizza, they mean Buddy’s Pizza. The restaurant has expanded to over a dozen locations over the years and is supposed to eventually go national. The restaurant on Conant is the original, the beloved, the often imitated but never (in the minds and hearts of Detroiters) surpassed. Plenty of toppings are available, but most go for the classic square pie baked into blue steel pans and topped with loads of melty, caramelized brick cheese, pepperoni, and a drizzle of red sauce on top.

places.name: Amar Pizza
places.address: 12195 Joseph Campau Street, Hamtramck, Michigan 48212
places.blurb: Hamtramck staple Amar Pizza fuses Bangladeshi, American, and Italian flavors into some really intriguing pies. While rounds and thin-crust pizzas are available, it’s hard to go wrong with Amar’s square pizza. Amar applies its own innovative twist on the old-school square by placing sauce underneath the cheese rather than on top, as well utilizing traditional Bangladeshi ingredients, spicy chicken naga, fry fish, and for the spice lovers, a fiery ghost pepper.

places.name: Shield’s Pizza
places.address: 5057 Woodward Avenue, MI 48202
places.blurb: Founded in Detroit in 1937, Shield’s is known for its pan pizzas. The metro area chain, which reestablished itself in Detroit proper in 2019, offers a classic version of the square with sauce on top of a generous helping of brick cheese.

places.name: Z’s Villa
places.address: 42 Piquette St, Detroit, MI, 48202, US
places.blurb: Tucked inside a big Victorian house in Milwaukee Junction, Z’s Villa is known for its massive outdoor area filled with patio games and beach volleyball. The square pan pizzas here are topped with a three-cheese blend and served whole or by-the-slice. Customize the pizza with toppings like pepperoni, kielbasa, hot peppers, and anchovies.

places.name: Grandma Bob’s Pizza
places.address: 2135 Michigan Avenue, Detroit, Michigan 48216
places.blurb: Shortly after opening, Grandma Bob’s embraced the Detroit-style pizza, and it quickly became a hit. Choose from options like sausage pistachio or a Hawaiian-style square with chorizo, pineapple, onions, and pickled jalapeño. The restaurant also features plenty of vegan options and rotating specials that tend to sell out quickly. One feature of Grandma Bob’s, is its Spread Dough charity effort, wherein every last Tuesday of each month, a local chef or other member of the city’s food scene is invited to create their own pizza recipe. All profits from the fundraiser go to a charity of that individual’s choice.

places.name: Pie-Sci
places.address: 5163 Trumbull St, Detroit, MI, 48208, US
places.blurb: Neighborhood pizzeria favorite Pie-Sci serves an eclectic selection of rotating pizza specials with punny names. Customers can order a round pizza here, but the square slices with crunchy corners and a buttery crust are superb. Creative options have included the Detroit Brussels Harder with garlic oil, mozzarella, oven-roasted Brussels sprouts, feta cheese, bacon, and balsamic glaze drizzle. Pie-Sci is also vegan-friendly.

places.name: Michigan & Trumbull
places.address: 1331 Holden St Apt 100, Detroit, MI, 48202, US
places.blurb: After just over three years situated in Corktown, Detroit-style pizzeria Michigan & Trumbull relocated in 2023 to the Elijah McCoy neighborhood, conveniently still just off of Trumbull. Customers can expect unconventional toppings and lots of vegan options. Take, for example, the McLovin’, a white pizza featuring ground beef, mozzarella, cheddar, fresh iceberg lettuce, and a shower of sesame seeds with “Awesome Sauce” that faithfully mimics a certain fast-food hamburger. Round out the meal with sides like Teader fries, deep-fried calzones known as Boblo Boats, and antipasto salad.

places.name: Loui’s Pizza
places.address: 23141 Dequindre Rd, Hazel Park, MI, 48030, US
places.blurb: Founded by a former pizza chef from Buddy’s and Shield’s, Loui’s in Hazel Park serves a hearty version of Detroit-style square pizza with a perfectly charred yet buttery crust, melted cheese, and robust sauce. Don’t forget to add an antipasto salad to the order and maybe a bottle of Chianti. For extra hungry diners, spring for the Loui’s Special, a behemoth barbecue Philly steak sandwich that will make for excellent leftovers later.

places.name: Sicily’s pizzeria and subs
places.address: 3554 W Vernor Hwy, Detroit, MI, 48216, US
places.blurb: The popular neighborhood restaurant on West Vernor in Southwest Detroit serves a version of the Detroit-style square pie with naturally leavened sourdough, but cut in the same fashion as a round pizza, giving visitors thick, triangle slices instead of little squares. The ranch-based Angry Bird is among the spot’s specialties, featuring hot chicken, beef bacon, jalapeños, and mozzarella. What’s more, the beloved pizzeria boasts a spacious dining room.

places.name: Green Lantern Pizza
places.address: 28960 John R Rd, Madison Heights, MI, 48071, US
places.blurb: This old-school pizza parlor mini-chain (which has nothing to do with the ring-slinging comic book character) goes back more than 60 years to when Thomas and Irene Vettraino bought an old farmhouse bar in Madison Heights, complete with a Prohibition-era green lantern. The restaurant serves three styles of pizza, including a Detroit-style pan pizza covered in a thick layer of melty cheese and perfectly curled roni cups with little pools of grease at the center. Unlike the traditional Detroit style, the sauce here is placed beneath the cheese rather than layered over the top.

places.name: Palazzo Di Pizza
places.address: 1220 E 11 Mile Rd, Royal Oak, MI, 48067, US
places.blurb: This carryout square pizza spot in Royal Oak was co-founded by the late World Champion pizza maker and Detroit Style Pizza Company operator Shawn Randazzo, who passed away in 2020 after a battle with cancer. Some say Randazzo helped spark the current Detroit-style pizza craze at U.S. restaurants by presenting the style on a national stage. The menu features a blend of traditional brick cheese and mozzarella with dollops of red sauce on top and fresh grated Parmesan. Toppings range from spicy peppers and sausage to chicken carbonara pizza, complete with bacon and alfredo sauce. A large pizza here comes in the form of two small pizzas for a total of eight crispy corners.

places.name: Como's Restaurant
places.address: 22812 Woodward Ave, Ferndale, MI, 48220, US
places.blurb: Como’s, a longtime Italian restaurant at the corner of Nine Mile and Woodward Avenue, underwent major renovations, returning in 2019 with trendy Detroit-style pizza. Chef Zack Sklar’s version of the square pan pizza uses a sourdough starter that’s fermented for three days to give it a light, spongy, flavorful crust that’s complemented by toppings such as hand-cut pepperonis and Grana Padano cheese. This spot has vegan and gluten-free options on the menu, too.

places.name: The Detroit Pizza Bar
places.address: 7316 W McNichols Rd, Detroit, MI, 48221, US
places.blurb: In spring 2022, Marcus Jones and Akunna Olumba brought halal Detroit-style pizza and soul food to Livernois and 6 Mile. The Coleman Young pizza is a neighborhood favorite with ingredients of shrimp, crabmeat, lobster, cheese, garlic, and white sauce. The pizzeria combines quick and full service with the chance to enjoy pies on the building’s rooftop terrace during the warmer months. The pizzeria also participates in a profit-sharing model with its staff, which is made up of residents of the neighborhood.

places.name: Uncle Andy’s Pizza
places.address: 28957 Woodward Ave, Berkley, MI, 48072, US
places.blurb: This Berkley carry-out-only shop serves Detroit-style pizza with various toppings. The bacon cheddar cheeseburger pizza features ground beef, bacon, cheddar cheese, and onions. A more classic approach is taken with the Supreme, topped with bacon, green peppers, ham, mushrooms, onions, pepperoni, and sausage. When building any pie, customers have the option to shift any topping to one side or the other or spread them across the entire pizza.

places.name: Pizzana
places.address: 11712 San Vincente Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90049, USA
places.blurb: Plenty has been said about chef Daniele Uditi’s neo-Neapolitan pies, but it’s best to head to one of the locations in Brentwood, Marina del Rey, Sherman Oaks, West Hollywood, or Silver Lake to try for yourself. Co-founded with Candace and Charles Nelson of Sprinkles, Caroline O’Donnell, and actor Chris O’Donnell, the budding chain of wood-fired pizza shops recently expanded to Dallas and Houston in Texas. The pizzas here employ seasonal ingredients and come in a variety of classic and new-school topping combinations. There’s classic pepperoni or margherita for first-timers but those who return will venture into the creamy cacio e pepe or the diavola topped with spicy salami and nduja walnut romesco. The unique elevated pan ensures slices are crisp until the last bite.

places.name: Cosetta
places.address: 3150 Ocean Park Boulevard, Santa Monica, California 90405
places.blurb: Chef Zach Pollack honed his approach to pizza after years of working the oven at Sotto and later the Italian American versions at his now-closed Echo Park restaurant Cosa Buona. Now with Cosetta, he’s doing blistered wood-fired 10-inch pizzas in a variety of creative topping combinations. The puffy crusts straddle a neo-Neapolitan and New York style, with a less craggly exterior and a more composed underbody. The results are something shareable and fun that likely travels well in delivery and takeout. The Peparazzi comes with a sizzle of spicy sopressata and vegetal Fresno chiles while the decidedly untraditional Hawaiian Sunrise gets house-smoked coppa, pineapple, onion, cilantro, and jalapeño. After sharing some slices, be sure to try the crispy-crunchy chicken, eggplant, or sand dab Milaneses, which can come gluten-free as well.

places.name: Pizza Wagon
places.address: 14522 1/2 Ventura Blvd, Sherman Oaks, CA 91403
places.blurb: The quick, super-affordable New York City-style pizza spot nails the crispy-bottomed slice that properly melds cheese with tomato sauce. The casual space churns out whole pies and slices, with delivery people dropping in every few minutes to take boxes out. The entire operation feels very much like a neighborhood Bronx or Brooklyn shop, which adds to those nostalgic for true New York-style pizza. Every pepperoni curls like a blooming flower, pooling together porcine juices that dribble onto the paper plate. Consider these slices the ideal way to cap off an evening where dinner leaves one still hungry.

places.name: Little Dynamite
places.address: 11736 Washington Pl, Los Angeles, CA 90066, USA
places.blurb: Former Bootleg Pizzeria operator Kyle Lambert is a deep-dish Detroit-style pizza specialist. His square slices are plush, made with naturally fermented dough that features an ever-so-slight tangy flavor. Lambert and partner Courtney Glowacz operate from a small takeout-only Culver City slot with a rock-and-roll vibe that resembles the camaraderie part of the Bear’s first season. The square pies are laden with cheese and personality and suit any mood. Slices are available during the day from noon to 3 p.m., with pies only from noon until 9 p.m., Wednesday to Sunday. Check the menu for some recent tavern-style thin-crust pizzas too.

places.name: Redwood Pie
places.address: 511 Pier Avenue, Hermosa Beach, California 90254
places.blurb: Perhaps the best pizzeria to open in the South Bay, Redwood Pie comes from a Sotto veteran and longtime baker Erik Vose, who also worked at Inglewood’s Cadoro bakery. Redwood’s New York-style pizzas come with familiar toppings like sausage and pepperoni, or soppressata with olives, but feature a flavorful sourdough crust based on a San Francisco yeast culture. The crust’s texture balances something chewy and slightly crispy, firm enough to hold up to the sauce, cheese, and toppings but light enough for an average person to eat half a large pizza. Vose named his pizzeria after redwood forests, but the ocean adjacent Hermosa Beach location feels a world away from massive trees. Maybe that’s the dichotomy Vose was going for with the crust: sourdough, artisanal flavors but shaped into a crisp, thin New York City-style 16-inch pie. The results are tremendous.

places.name: Pizzeria Sei
places.address: 8781 W Pico Blvd (Robertson Blvd), Los Angeles, CA 90035
places.blurb: William Joo trained at Providence and Ronan before opening his own Tokyo-style Neapolitan pizzeria in Pico-Robertson. Joo and wife Jennifer So operate this minimalist gem with a dedicated team serving a tight array of wood-fired pizzas boasting chewy, mochi-like crusts. Italian standards like margherita, marinara, and a caper-and-anchovy-topped Napoletana share menu space with more envelope-pushing pies like Joo’s Castelvetrano olive- and sopressata-topped Diavola and the prosciutto cotto-, egg-, and truffle-oil adorned Bismarck. Sei continues to garner attention for its understated excellence. Pizza enthusiasts can try to get a counter seat at Joo’s monthly omakase, a pricey but truly spectacular adventure into the mind of a pizza chef.

places.name: Ozzy’s Apizza
places.address: 5300 Lankershim Boulevard, North Hollywood, California 91601
places.blurb: This New Haven pizza specialist has settle in to a strip mall storefront in North Hollywood. Expect blistered thin-crust pies topped with all the familiar classics, including pepperoni, ricotta cream with sausage, and clams. The meatlover’s Swanson pizza, inspired by Nick Offerman’s character in Parks & Rec, comes with bacon, sausage, pepperoni, and a thick layer of mozzarella cheese. Ozzy’s has become successful enough that it has opened a location in its spiritual home city of New Haven, likely the first time a restaurant in Los Angeles has ventured there. Ozzy’s also operates a second location in Glendale.

places.name: Fiorelli Pizza
places.address: 8236 W 3rd St, Los Angeles, CA 90048
places.blurb: After a stint at Cook’s Garden in Venice, Michael Fiorelli and business partner Liz Gutierrez have moved their pizza operation into a permanent home on West Third Street. At the restaurant, the duo slings pies that combine New York and Neapolitan styles, with a blistered crust and soft undercarriage. Opt for the crowd-pleasing pepperoni with curled pepperoni cups, or the supreme with a mix of mushrooms, sausage, pepperoni, onions, red bell peppers, and jalapeño. Round out meals with a marinated olives, Caesar salad, and lamb Bolognese.

places.name: Dtown Pizzeria
places.address: 8424 Santa Monica Blvd, West Hollywood, CA, 90069, US
places.blurb: For a full-on Detroit pie experience, Dtown Pizzeria is a must. Made by native Michigander Ryan Ososky, these hefty pies come with the sauce on top, the stretchy cheese, and all the crusty-cornered deliciousness one could want. Dtown’s West Hollywood shop does the classic pepperoni and sauce version to a tee, but creativity abounds here, including an award-winning shaved A5 wagyu tenderloin pizza with caviar, black truffle oil, and pickled red onions for $99. A Hawaiian luau-themed vegan pizza featured charred pineapple and barbecue sauce for something so far off from the original Detroit style that it might appropriate be called “California square pizza.” Those who want a gluten-free crust will find that option on every pizza for an additional $5, and those who want a pre-pizza snack will find that the dry-rub smoked wings and honey lemon pepper wings are a cut above the average slice shop.

places.name: Ronan
places.address: 7315 Melrose Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90046, USA
places.blurb: Sotto veteran Daniel Cutler oversees this pizza and small plates restaurant with his wife and general manager Caitlin Cutler. With inventive pizzas boasting supple but charred crusts and levain bread with a wonderfully brown crust, Ronan is a haven for those who love carbohydrates. There are 10 different topping combinations, but the Super Marionario with anchovies, garlic, and tomato sauce is a highlight. The dining room feels deeply moody and perfect any night, and the cocktails are terrific too.

places.name: Apollonia's Pizzeria
places.address: 5176 Wilshire Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90036, USA
places.blurb: It’s hard to think of a more photogenic pizza than the square Detroit-style pies from the unassuming Apollonia’s. These dense, expertly cheesy pizzas are filled with crunch, cheese, and plenty of sauce, and they might even be a little bit salty for some folks (take that as a warning). But the strip mall shop’s crispy, thin, round pies are also stellar, reflecting a pure New York City slice parlor in the heart of Los Angeles’s Mid-Wilshire area. Note the limited hours as it’s closed Mondays and Tuesdays, and it’s only open for lunch and early evening service from Wednesday to Sunday. Apollonia’s also operates a second location in Crenshaw Square.

places.name: Old Gold Tomato Pies
places.address: 4681 Hollywood Boulevard, Los Angeles, California 90027, United States
places.blurb: After years of popping up around town, chef Jeff Vance’s Sicilian pizza project Old Gold Tomato Pies finally has a permanent home in Los Feliz. The pizzeria opened on a busy stretch of Hollywood Boulevard in late October with a menu of full pies and slices that includes plenty of vegan options. The North Beach gets topped with anchovies, roasted peppers, and Castelvetrano olives, while the signature Old Gold offers four cheeses and tomatoes. Vegan options are topped with dairy-free cheese, vegan pepperoni, and more. To take the slices to the next level, add on vegan tofu ranch, ricotta, honey, or anchovies.

places.name: Pijja Palace
places.address: 2711 Sunset Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90026, USA
places.blurb: This sports bar-meets-Indian-pizza parlor has quickly built a following for shareable pies. Choose a base sauce from makhini (made with tomatoes, spices, and cream), a peri-peri vindaloo (made with a roux and Kashmiri red chiles, and fortified with rich vegetable stock), or white korma (sweet onions, yogurt, and spices). Toppings include homemade Goan sausage, roasted Fresnos, and chicken tikka for a choose-your-own flavor adventure.

places.name: Quarter Sheets Pizza
places.address: 1305 Portia St, Los Angeles, CA 90026, USA
places.blurb: What started as a pandemic pop-up has become a wildly successful standalone pizza-and-cake restaurant in Echo Park. Aaron Lindell and Hannah Ziskin are a match made in pizza-cake heaven; Lindell makes Detroit-style pizzas while Ziskin zeroes in on some of the best desserts in town, which are layered slab cakes, princess cakes, and thick slices of mud pie. The place now takes reservations which makes it easier for those who plan ahead, but walk-ins can still try to brave the crowds during meal times.

places.name: Danny Boy's Famous Original Pizza
places.address: 330 S. Hope Street, Suite 205, Los Angeles, CA 90071, USA
places.blurb: Danny Boy’s comes from one of New York City’s former Meatball Shop co-founders and serves as an homage to East Coast red-sauce roots. It’s in an unusual space, tucked away in an office building atrium, a lovely place to have takeout pizza. Recently the shop has become very popular on TikTok, leading to long lines during lunch hour. The shop expanded to Westwood just in time for college students to upgrade their delivery pizza during study sessions.

places.name: Pizzeria Bianco
places.address: 1320 E 7th St #100, Los Angeles, CA 90021, USA
places.blurb: Pizzeria Bianco dishes out whole pies and daytime slices alike. The whole wood-fired pizzas have been celebrated since the 2000s, when chef Chris Bianco opened its Phoenix pizzeria to great acclaim. Some of the signature pizzas include the Rosa, with red onion, pistachio, rosemary, and parmesan cheese; and the Sonny Boy, sporting wide soppressata, Gaeta olives, and fresh mozzarella cheese. Bianco now splits his time between Los Angeles and Phoenix, and he’s even growing his brand locally with Pane Bianco for slices and sandwiches nearby at the same Row DTLA complex.

places.name: LaSorted’s Chinatown
places.address: 984 N Broadway, Los Angeles, CA, 90012, US
places.blurb: This off-shoot of Silver Lake’s New York-inspired slice shop takes owner Tommy Brockert’s vision to a full-service space. Decked wall-to-wall with Los Angeles sports memorabilia, the photographer and pizza aficionado makes large New York City slices with a sourdough crust, topped with Alta Cucina tomato sauce, pepperoni, mushrooms, burrata, and sausage. Brockert’s Chinatown location gives an ’80s pizzeria vibe with breaded zucchini, chicken wings, salads, hoagies, and fries to accompany the pizzas.

places.name: Wallflour Pizza
places.address: 2128 Colorado Boulevard, Los Angeles, California 90041
places.blurb: Opened as Quarantine Pizza during the pandemic, this sourdough pizzeria from Brandon Conaway and Carolina Pedroza-Conaway debuted in a bright Eagle Rock space in April serving a tight menu of 14-inch pizzas that are large enough for two hungry adults to share. The family-friendly space serves five pizzas, including pepperoni and cheese; spicy pepperoni with jalapeño and hot honey, mushrooms with garlic cream, and a marinara-style tomato sauce with garlic confit. Throw in a few salads, sourdough cookie, and pandan olive cake, and that’s basically the entire menu. Staff will likely upsell the $2 side of ranch; luckily, the dip is a pretty great way to polish off those tangy naturally fermented crusts.

places.name: Bub & Grandma’s Pizza
places.address: 5101 York Boulevard, Los Angeles, California 90042, United States
places.blurb: Sourdough specialists Bub and Grandma’s expanded with Bub and Grandma’s Pizza, an East Coast-style sourdough slice shop in the former Highland Park Town Pizza. The pizza at Bub & Grandma’s takes inspiration from the East Coast pies of Boston, New Haven, New York, and New Jersey, including those served at Zuppardi’s, Sally’s Apizza, Star Tavern, and Frank Pepe’s. Slices may change by the day, but classics like a cheese slice, a pepperoni slice, and a vegan red pie slice will always be available. Full pies include the Potato White with potato, caramelized onion, Taleggio, mozzarella, Parmesan, and fried rosemary, and the Bolognese that uses red sauce, beef and pork ragu, mozzarella, and Parmesan to euphoric effect. Alongside the pizzas, the restaurant serves meatballs with marinara sauce, supplì, chicken wings, and garlic knots, as well as a Caesar salad with gem lettuce. The pizzeria is currently take-out only with dine-in coming in the future.

places.name: Secret Pizza
places.address: 3501 Monterey Road, Los Angeles, California 90032
places.blurb: During the pandemic, Sean Lango started Secret Pizza from his apartment. Secret Pizza sells out regularly and here’s why: the New Jersey-born operator bakes what he calls “East Coast-style” pizzas bearing simple toppings on quality dough. Most folks will start out with the wide New York-style pies but Lango also offers a Sicilian and thinner square grandma laced with mozzarella, basil, and spoonfuls of tomato sauce. Pre-order to make sure one can secure a pie from this El Sereno spot.

places.name: Prime Pizza
places.address: 1900 Allen Ave, Altadena, CA, 91001, US
places.blurb: Prime Pizza has become Los Angeles’s de facto slice joint. The place not only does simple thin round pies, but it also makes square and Sicilian-style pizzas daily. The New York City-style slices are wide, thin, and well-balanced with ample toppings and floppy crusts. Topping combinations embrace California sensibility too, like the loaded barbecue chicken pizza or veggie pie that comes with mushrooms, garlic, red peppers, radicchio, and kale. Prime Pizza has shops across Los Angeles, including in Burbank, near Santa Monica College, Fairfax District, West Los Angeles, El Segundo, and Altadena.

places.name: Steve's Pizza
places.address: 12010 Biscayne Blvd, Miami, FL 33181, USA
places.blurb: Steve’s Pizza has slung pies since 1974, and it shows in the best way. The place looks like it hasn’t been renovated in decades — stickers dot the booths, Polaroids of beloved customers cover the order counter, and there’s a genuine sense of community that’s hard to find these days. It’s the kind of spot that kept serving the neighborhood during hurricanes, even if it meant doing it by candlelight. The pizza? Classic American style. Doughy crust with some heft, a slightly sweet sauce, and cheese that pools into greasy goodness, whether it’s a classic or Sicilian slice. It might not change your life, but it’s guaranteed to hit the spot, whether you’re dropping in for a cheap $4.50 lunch bite or packing a table with friends late into the night.

places.name: Stanzione
places.address: 8300 NE 2nd Ave, Miami, FL, 33138, US
places.blurb: Stanzione 87, the longtime Brickell staple, made a triumphant return to the Citadel in Little River in the same stand where its sister restaurant, Ash, used to be. Known for its dedication to Neapolitan-style pizza, it brought back favorites like the classic Margherita, chopped salad, and the crispy sous-vide wings dripping in Calabrian chili oil. We could spend a lifetime chewing into the carbonara, which is everything you love about the hearty pasta dish but on a pizza with a bubbling charred crust. We dare you to share — mostly so you can save room for the oozy Nutella calzone.

places.name: Ironside Pizza
places.address: 7580 NE 4th Ct, Miami, FL 33138, USA
places.blurb: Ironside Pizza, located in Miami’s creative Ironside complex, combines perfectly charred, Neapolitan-style pizzas with a breezy, industrial courtyard lined with tropical trees. It’s the kind of spot that works for everything from casual family dinners to long, wine-filled lunches with friends. Once a BYOB place, it now touts a thoughtful wine list and pairs beautifully with favorites like the pizza Regina, topped with fresh arugula, tomatoes, and prosciutto, or the rich, six-week-marinated bolognese so good, a nonna might raise her nose out of intimidation.

places.name: Bar Bucce
places.address: 7220 North Miami Avenue, Miami, Florida 33150
places.blurb: Bar Bucce is part pizzeria, part bottle shop, and part Italian deli from the award-winning team behind Macchialina. The space is big, bright, and constantly in motion, with indoor tables surrounded by shelves of wine, specialty liqueurs, and hip Italian provisions, and a patio that hums with neighborhood regulars and the occasional freight train rolling by. The dough ferments for 72 hours before hitting the oven, and pizzas emerge lightly charred and topped with combinations like rock shrimp and sweet onion, or zucchini with egg jam. Come with friends and order a chinato-laced Pitcher O’Cola, the Italian answer to a Tinto de Verano.

places.name: ‘O Munaciello
places.address: 6425 Biscayne Blvd, Miami, FL, 33138, US
places.blurb: The first tip-off that O’Munnaciello was something special? Nearly every mismatched fluorescent table was filled with Italians. O’Munaciello was founded by Florentines so obsessed with Naples, they brought a taste of it to Florence. They later chose Miami as the second home for its Neapolitan-inspired pies, and now count locations in MiMo and on Coral Way. Recently, it’s been racking up tons of accolades, including #11 on the list of 50 Top Pizza Restaurants in the U.S. by 50 Top Pizza. And one bite into the Maradona, featuring a burrata that oozes when cut, or the Cuore di Pistacchio, a more refined pie made with a mild style of mozzarella and topped with almost translucent slices of mortadella and no shortage of pistachio crumbles and cream, will tell you exactly why.

places.name: Francesco Martucci
places.address: 10 Northeast 27th Street, Miami, Florida 33137, United States
places.blurb: Francesco Martucci’s Miami debut brings one of the world’s most decorated pizzaiolos to Wynwood, and it’s no slice shop. The namesake restaurant translates the experience of his legendary I Masanielli in Caserta into a sleek, design-forward space where pizza is treated less like comfort food and more like a tasting menu. The dough is the main event: long-fermented and feather-light, with a tall, airy canotto crust that looks substantial but feels weightless. The Marinara Futuro, a refined take on the classic marinara pie with multiple tomato preparations, shows Martucci’s philosophy best: familiar flavors, elevated through intricate technique.

places.name: Joe's Pizza NYC
places.address: 234 NW 25th St, Miami, FL 33127, USA
places.blurb: For years, plenty of Miami spots claimed to make the best New York-style pizza in town — and then along came Joe’s. This is the Greenwich Village OG, serving near-perfect triangles of thin, crispy crust with just enough chew, leaving customers with a coating of semolina dust on their fingers to sprinkle around Wynwood and South Beach. The classic cheese slice is all you need: tangy sauce, gooey mozzarella, and that iconic foldable base. Open late, it’s the go-to for a quick single-slice fix.

places.name: Lucali
places.address: 1930 Bay Rd, Miami Beach, FL 33139, USA
places.blurb: Lucali in South Beach’s Sunset Harbour is the Miami outpost of the Brooklyn institution, serving what many argue is the best pizza in town. The signature pie is a 20-inch masterpiece: a perfectly charred thin crust topped with a rich pomodoro sauce, a mix of gooey cheeses, and fresh basil. The ambiance is as much a draw as the pizza itself. With candlelit tables, an open kitchen, and a warm, rustic vibe, it’s the perfect spot for a laid-back night. While you wait (because there’s always a wait), head next door to the Bay Club for a cocktail or two.

places.name: Miami Slice
places.address: 1335 NE Miami Ct, Miami, FL 33132, USA
places.blurb: Miami Slice feels like one of those places you don’t want to tell too many people about — but the secret’s already out. Tucked into a low-key spot Downtown — with a secondary location in Key Biscayne — this restaurant with a cult following slings New York-style pies and slices with a South Florida edge. The vibe is laid back but intentional, with just a few counter seats where customers can watch the pizza magic happen. Try La Salsera, a combination of red sauce, vodka sauce, and a generous swirl of pesto that dances on your palate as though it’s listening to Willy Colón. Then, spin a white pie into the mix; the Leeks On Bacon, with garlic confit cream, will remain on your breath and in your memory for a long time.

places.name: Eleventh Street Pizza
places.address: 1035 N Miami Ave, Miami, FL 33136, USA
places.blurb: While this list is about pizza, it’s hard to talk about Eleventh Street without mentioning the garlic knots. Unlike the chewy, greasy ones you probably grew up with, these dense sourdough versions are blanketed by paper-thin Parmesan shreds and won’t pierce your throat with an acidic sting of garlic. Pair them with the venue’s signature pork and beef meatballs or spicy Caesar salad, and it’s almost a complete meal. Of course, you’re coming here for pizza, and these guys have the whole pepperoni and hot honey thing locked down. Customers love the seasonal, limited edition pizzas, whether it’s a caviar and potato pie or a summery release with blueberries baked in. The downtown location is open until 4 a.m., and it’s best suited for pickup and quick munching, while the Dadeland venue is great for family-friendly, sit-down dining.

places.name: ViceVersa
places.address: 398 Northeast 5th Street, Miami, Florida 33132
places.blurb: No one forgets a bad first date, but in this case, it’s actually a pizza topped with charred eggplant, tomato sauce, and peppers. Vice Versa is best known for its Italian aperitivo and cocktail program, but the restaurant also specializes in neo-Neapolitan creations like a shellfish pizza with Middleneck clams, and possibly the best mushroom pizza in all of Miami. The dough undergoes up to 48 hours of cold fermentation, using local flour. You might come to Vice Versa for the Negronis, but you should always leave with a pie.

places.name: Fratesi’s Pizza
places.address: 69 East Flagler Street, Miami, Florida 33131, United States
places.blurb: Between the Tiffany-style lights, tomato sauce-hued walls, and tight vinyl booths, Fratesi’s might spark nostalgia for Pizza Hut. That thought disappears the moment a pie hits the table. After building a cult following through its Over Under pop-up, Fratesi’s landed a permanent home just a block west on Flagler serving slices so thin you could slide them under a door. Of course, that would be sacrilege; respect must be paid to a pizza whose trinity of cheeses bubble so luxuriously over the edges. The Carmelita, Fratesi’s take on a margherita, is a classic for a reason, but regulars swear by the Demon Pig Boy, loaded with pepperoni cups and enough jalapeños to make Hades sweat. Don’t skip the tahini Caesar either — deeply nutty, piled high with parmesan and breadcrumbs, and the perfect companion to the hot pies.

places.name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza (Multiple locations)
places.address: 1000 S Miami Ave, Miami, FL 33130, USA
places.blurb: Mister 01 might have locations all over South Florida (as well as Texas, and Georgia, and North Carolina), but it feels anything but chain-y. Its thin, Neapolitan-style crust is distinctly light, made with yeast imported from a small farm in Italy and formed into a dough that ferments for at least 72 hours. While the classic pizzas are magnets for a return visit, the true standouts at Mister 01 are the star-shaped pies, with crusts pinched into five-point arrangements and prodded with generous dollops of ricotta cheese. If it’s your first time here, go with the Star Luca, which is like a margherita dotted with juicy Calabrian salami. If you want something unique, the Star Beckham (yes, named after that Miami resident) has a labyrinth-like arrangement of bacon, onions, and ranch drizzle — AKA everything you like about football (or fútbol) Sundays.

places.name: Vice City Pizza
places.address: 2615 SW 147th Ave, Miami, FL 33175, USA
places.blurb: Vice City Pizza started as a pandemic pop-up and has now planted roots in a cozy 1,200-square-foot spot way out in West Kendall. The fluffy, sauce-drenched interior of the specialty Detroit-style pizzas is fenced in by a cracker-like crust; the pizzas look hefty, but each bite is surprisingly light. Upgrade from the usual hot honey pepperoni and go for the Ron Swanson, which is also loaded with thick slabs of bacon, banana pepper, and fennel sausage. If you’re feeling adventurous, try the fig and brie pizza, which is what happens when a pizza party meets a grown-up soiree.

places.name: The Big Cheese of Miami
places.address: 8080 SW 67th Ave, Miami, FL 33143, USA
places.blurb: Known as the Official Pizza of the U, the Big Cheese of Miami has seen it all — from championship wins to Heisman trophies and countless late-night celebrations. The walls are a shrine to Hurricanes history, packed with University of Miami memorabilia, jerseys, photos, and headlines that make it clear: this place bleeds orange and green. The ambiance is no-frills — think Formica tables, soda counter stools, buzzing TVs playing sports highlights, and a crowd that’s equal parts UM students, alumni, and locals biting into what may be the cheesiest slices in all of Miami. It also offers a lineup of calzones, wings, hearty subs, and glossy garlic knots that generations of pizza lovers have loved since 1984.

places.name: Maxwell Bros Clothing Store
places.address: 17395 S Dixie Hwy, Palmetto Bay, FL, 33157, US
places.blurb: From a family whose clothing business dates back to the 1860s comes… a pizza shop in Palmetto Bay. Maxwell Bros. trades history for pure mid-century kitsch, where wood paneling, vintage signs, and old-school lighting give the whole place the feel of a retro suburban hangout. The Neapolitan-style pies land somewhere between classic American comfort and careful craft. The crust bakes up crisp on the bottom with enough chew to support generous layers of cheese and toppings. Start with the pepperoni, where the edges curl and char into savory little cups, or go for a house specialty like the G.O.A.T, with goat cheese, sauteed spinach, and a curious drizzle of Thai-style sweet chile sauce.

places.name: Gracie’s Apizza
places.address: 7304 North Leavitt Avenue, Portland, Oregon 97203
places.blurb: At Gracie’s Apizza, a rye sourdough starter imparts a noteworthy tang to Pacific Northwest grain establishing the canvas for a rotating selection of fun touches or seasonal treats. Pies drizzled with Oregon green garlic arrive alongside pizzas topped with a handful of hazelnuts and homemade mozzarella, accompanied by a classic, intentionally simple tomato pie; for those looking for something different, Gracie’s calzone is also one of the city’s finest.

places.name: Lombardo’s Pizzeria
places.address: 5210 North Lombard Street, Portland, Oregon 97203
places.blurb: The interior of Lombardos is airy and austere, a lot of glass and cement and cans of tomatoes, with little in the way of seating. The minimalist decor speaks to the intense focus on the product, and the results more than make up for a lack of battered vinyl booths. Lombardo’s does North Jersey pies, including cracker-thin bar-style and traditionally inverted tomato pie (the sauce goes on top of the cheese for a deeply roasted burst of tomato). For extra seating, visit the Chill and Fill cart pod across the street, or camp out at nearby Philly bar Lombard House for the true East Coast experience.

places.name: Paladin Pie
places.address: 1027 Northeast Alberta Street, Portland, Oregon 97211
places.blurb: This Alberta pizza cart from Oven & Shaker alum Leo Brill is a distinct departure from the Pearl District pizzeria’s pies.
